original_user_request: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game in a single HTML file using Three.js from the CDN, featuring lanes, obstacles, a player character, scoring, and controls.
precise_rewrite: Create an HTML file that includes Three.js from a CDN to develop a 3D Crossy Road game. The game should have lanes, obstacles, a player character, a scoring system, and controllable movement.
assumptions: The user wants a single HTML file solution.
Three.js will be used for the 3D rendering.
The CDN version of Three.js will be utilized.

tradeoffs.name: Speed vs Quality
tradeoffs.rationale: Balancing between development speed and the quality of the game features.

tradeoffs.name: Realism vs Fun
tradeoffs.rationale: Deciding whether to prioritize realistic 3D graphics or a more fun, simplified gameplay experience.

tradeoffs.name: Security vs Cost
tradeoffs.rationale: Evaluating the trade-off between ensuring the game is secure and minimizing costs.

speed.rationale: The user likely wants a quick solution, so prioritizing speed is important.

quality.rationale: While quality is crucial, the user may be willing to compromise slightly for speed.

realism.rationale: Realism is less critical than fun and quality, but still important.

fun.rationale: The game should be enjoyable, so maximizing fun is a priority.

security.rationale: Security is important, but not as critical as speed and fun. The CDN version of Three.js is generally secure.

cost.rationale: Cost should be considered, but the user may be willing to spend more for a higher-quality game.

definition_of_done: A player character is designed and integrated.
Controllable movement for the player character.
non_goals: Incorporating advanced physics or complex AI.
Developing a multiplayer version of the game.
Creating an offline version that requires installation.
conflict_rules: If there is a conflict between speed and quality, prioritize quality to ensure the game is enjoyable and well-designed.
If security and cost are in conflict, prioritize security as it is crucial for user trust and data protection.
If realism and fun are in conflict, prioritize fun to maintain an engaging gameplay experience.
